message:
fix(fp-io): gate SQL ident helpers on sql-sqlite feature (br-ld8h / fd90.45)

Self-review surfaced a real --no-default-features build defect:
escape_sql_ident and quote_sql_ident are private helpers used only
from the rusqlite SqlConnection impl (cfg-gated to sql-sqlite), but
the helpers themselves were unconditional. Building fp-io with
--no-default-features emitted dead-code warnings for both fns,
which would trip clippy -D warnings in CI for that profile.

Fix: add #[cfg(feature = "sql-sqlite")] to both helper definitions
so they're only compiled when the rusqlite path is also compiled.
No external API change — these are private fns.

Verified: clippy --no-default-features -- -D warnings now passes
clean. Default-features build still passes 414 tests with clippy
clean. ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-lblu","title":"Add Series dt to_timestamp/time conformance coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:59:10.942585448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T19:01:39.996583300Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T19:01:39.993916734Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-lbsx","title":"fp-types Timestamp string-unit rounding (9p0u Phase 2.6)","description":"Phase 2.6 follow-up to br-9p0u + br-5h6n. Pandas-style string-unit rounding on Timestamp + promote `Timedelta::unit_to_nanos` to public.

**Scope.**
- Promote `Timedelta::unit_to_nanos(unit)` from private to `pub`. Already implements the pandas alias set (W/D/H/m/s/ms/us/ns + word-form aliases).
- Add three string-unit rounding methods on Timestamp:
  - `Timestamp::floor_to_unit(unit_str) -> Timestamp`
  - `Timestamp::ceil_to_unit(unit_str) -> Timestamp`
  - `Timestamp::round_to_unit(unit_str) -> Timestamp`
- Each looks up `Timedelta::unit_to_nanos(unit_str)`; on unknown unit returns NaT (matches pandas ValueError surface as NaT propagation, consistent with the rest of fp-types).

**Reference (pandas 2.2):**
- `pd.Timestamp('2024-01-01 12:34:56').floor('H')` → 12:00:00.
- `.ceil('1H')` → 13:00:00.
- `.round('H')` → 13:00:00 (banker's tie-to-even).
- Unknown unit: pandas raises `ValueError: <X> is not a fixed frequency`.

**Fix shape (purely additive in fp-types):**
- 1 visibility change (`fn unit_to_nanos` → `pub fn unit_to_nanos`).
- 3 new pub fns on Timestamp delegating to the existing nanos-based variants.
- Tests covering pandas-canonical units + word forms + unknown unit → NaT.

**Blast radius.** No call-site touches (private→public is backwards-compat). Zero dep-graph change. fp-types stays chrono-free.

**Severity.** LOW — UX polish on top of 5h6n.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T06:08:31.785090190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T06:11:19.962871273Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T06:11:19.960395778Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dtype","parity-gap","review","timestamp"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-ld8h","title":"fd90.45: gate escape_sql_ident / quote_sql_ident on sql-sqlite to silence no-default-features dead-code warnings","description":"Building fp-io with --no-default-features (i.e. without sql-sqlite) emits dead-code warnings for escape_sql_ident and quote_sql_ident — they're only used from the rusqlite SqlConnection impl which is cfg-gated to sql-sqlite. The helpers themselves are unconditional. Under cargo clippy --no-default-features, these warnings will trip clippy -D warnings if anyone runs that build profile in CI. Fix: add #[cfg(feature = "sql-sqlite")] to both helper definitions so they're only compiled when the feature is on. No external API change — these are private fns.  {"id":"frankenpandas-liai","title":"[MEDIUM] fuzz: no differential-vs-pandas target despite live pandas oracle being available","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill's Oracle Hierarchy audit.

**Context:** crates/fp-conformance/oracle/pandas_oracle.py is a live pandas reference implementation. It already answers arbitrary operation payloads (see the dispatch at pandas_oracle.py:4615+). The `capture_live_oracle_expected` machinery in fp-conformance lib.rs can call it for any fixture. br-fmmq + br-28y8 + br-339q + br-icm3 + br-i2j8 already exploit this via assert_live_oracle_* tests.

**Gap:** NONE of the 21 fuzz targets use the pandas oracle as an oracle. Every target today uses either:
- Crash-only (fuzz_*_io_bytes: no panic + format round-trip)
- Invariant-only (fuzz_semantic_eq: reflexivity + symmetry + null-bridge)
- Cross-format round-trip (fuzz_format_cross_round_trip)

Per skill's Oracle Hierarchy (ranked strongest → weakest):
  1. Reference implementation  ← AVAILABLE, UNUSED
  2. Simplified shadow model
  3. Inverse operation          ← currently used
  4. Metamorphic relation
  5. Crash oracle               ← currently used

**Fix shape:** a new fuzz_differential_vs_pandas target that:
1. Reads a byte input, projects it into a tiny DataFrame (reuse fuzz_*_frame_from_bytes helpers).
2. Picks an operation from a byte tag (sum/mean/groupby-sum/merge/resample/rolling/...).
3. Calls the Rust path AND dispatches the same payload through pandas_oracle.py subprocess (or inline via PyO3 / a local oracle cache).
4. Asserts frame_a.semantic_eq(frame_b).

This catches the entire class of 'Rust produces a different answer than pandas' drift — the exact drift br-r4au / br-ch4o / br-pxv5 had to chase by hand.

Performance: oracle IPC is slow (~50ms/call), so target exec/s floor is 100 (stateful category, per skill), not 1000. Acceptable.

**Variants to consider** (file as children under this bead):
- fuzz_diff_groupby_vs_pandas (pin the multi-key emit from br-1zzp.2)
- fuzz_diff_merge_vs_pandas   (pin AG-05 alignment)
- fuzz_diff_eval_vs_pandas    (string expr engine; catches eval divergence)
- fuzz_diff_rolling_vs_pandas (pin std/var edge cases from br-fmmq)

No blocker. No current bug — just strongest-oracle-available isn't used.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:45:25.262617890Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:57:21.063439Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:57:21.063439Z","close_reason":"Phase 1 design: DIFFERENTIAL_FUZZ_DESIGN.md pins op alphabet, subprocess-overhead budget, nightly-only scheduling, and 4-phase roadmap. Implementation (long-lived oracle + harness) is Phase 2 work.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","fuzz","oracle","review"]}
